headline: Conflict of interest rules exempt 3 categories of senior officials
context: Malaysia's conflict of interest regulations under General Orders exempt three categories of senior officials including ministers, deputy ministers, and political secretaries.

cards.big: Conflict of interest rules apply to civil servants but exempt ministers entirely
cards.sub: The most powerful decision-makers face the weakest integrity requirements

cards.big: General Orders Chapter D applies only to public officers — not political appointees
cards.sub: Constitutional separation means ministers are not classified as public officers

cards.big: 5 sitting ministers have declared business interests in sectors they regulate
cards.sub: Direct financial interests in regulated industries create undisclosed conflicts

cards.big: Canada's Conflict of Interest Act covers all ministers and requires blind trusts
cards.sub: Comparable Westminster systems apply stricter rules to political leaders, not weaker

cards.big: When the rules exempt the most powerful, the rules exist to control the least powerful
Should a Ministerial Code of Conduct with binding conflict of interest rules be enacted?
